campbell.title: Campbell City Council — July 7, 2026
campbell.summary: Campbell City Council held a public hearing on July 7, 2026 to consider revoking business licenses and tobacco retailer permits for two smoke shops in the city, and to adopt a new interim ordinance related to Senate legislation. The council also reviewed consultant service agreements and a public works grant application.
keyTopics: Revocation of business licenses for California Cigar and Hayes Smoke Shop
New interim ordinance superseding previous tobacco-related regulations
Public Works grant submission authorization
Consultant service agreements approval

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — May 6, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council approved preliminary engineering reports and moved forward with a public hearing process for an upcoming ordinance vote scheduled for May 20th. The council also discussed whether the city should pursue charter city status and reviewed potential tax and revenue options.
keyTopics: Engineer's Report preliminary approval for fiscal year
Public hearing process underway
Ordinance scheduled for adoption vote on May 20, 2026
Charter city status consideration and direction to staff
Review of potential tax options and revenue estimates

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos Town Council — June 16, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos Town Council met on June 16, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from recent sessions and authorize the Town Manager to sign several agreements. The council approved contract amendments and new software services deals to keep town operations running smoothly.
keyTopics: Approved minutes from June 2 and June 8 council meetings
Authorized new five-year software services agreement
Approved amendments to legal services and litigation contracts
Town Manager given authority to execute multiple vendor agreements

san-jose.title: San José City Council — June 23, 2026
san-jose.summary: San José City Council met on June 23, 2026, to discuss several major items including a landmark designation for a historic building on South Sixth Street, a proposal to allow ranked choice voting for mayoral and council vacancies, advertising signage at San José airport, and a long-term power purchase agreement with Shell Energy North America.
keyTopics: Historic preservation: City Landmark Designation and Mills Act contract for 647 South Sixth Street property
Voting reform: Proposed charter amendment to enable ranked choice voting for filling mayor and council vacancies
Airport development: Decision on large format advertising sign contract at San José Mineta International Airport
Energy procurement: Long-term power purchase agreement with Shell Energy North America

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — June 23, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met to handle budget matters for the upcoming fiscal year, approve water management and infrastructure plans, and honor local organizations and a departing city attorney. The council also discussed two pending legal cases and various capital projects around the city.
keyTopics: Two pending lawsuits against the city requiring legal strategy discussions
FY 2026-27 budget and spending limits approved
Water shortage plans and 5-year water management update adopted
City Attorney Jennifer Logue recognized for appointment to the judiciary
Capital projects underway: Public Safety Building, golf course parking lot repaving, and salt pond restoration

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — June 23, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special closed-session meeting on June 23, 2026, followed by a regular public council meeting at 7 P.M. Closed sessions are private meetings where the council discusses sensitive matters that cannot be held in public.
keyTopics: Closed session convened (specific topics not publicly disclosed)
Regular council meeting held at 7 P.M.
Flag salute and public session

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — July 7, 2026
cupertino.summary: The City Council held a study session to review and update Cupertino's Health and Safety Element, which guides city planning through 2040. The council also recognized the Parks and Recreation Department for their work across multiple divisions and celebrated Monta Vista High School's Science Olympiad Team achievement.
keyTopics: Updating Health and Safety Element of the General Plan (2015-2040)
Parks and Recreation Department recognition for July Parks and Recreation Month
Monta Vista High School Science Olympiad Team achievement recognition

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — June 30, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council met on June 30, 2026, using a hybrid format that allowed residents to participate both in-person and remotely via Zoom. The city provided multiple ways for the public to attend and submit comments on agenda items.
keyTopics: Hybrid meeting format available (in-person and Zoom participation)
Public comment submission via eComment system on City Council Agenda page
Remote participation options: Zoom link and phone dial-in number provided

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to review and approve several administrative items, including updates to the city's federal housing and community development funding plan (Consolidated Plan), changes to how council vacancies are filled, and approval of meeting minutes. The council also confirmed its calendar of upcoming meetings and previewed items on the agenda for their next session.
keyTopics: Updates to the 2025-2030 Consolidated Plan for federal housing and development funding
Changes to the Council Vacancy Ordinance
Approval of meeting minutes and confirmation of council meeting schedule
New workplan requests submitted by individual council members

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — June 10, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to handle routine business including approving meeting schedules and assignments. The main item was voting on a new rule that changes the required setback (the distance buildings must be set back from the street) to 20 feet along a portion of Hansen Way at 3300 El Camino Real.
keyTopics: New 20-foot setback requirement for Hansen Way at 3300 El Camino Real
Approval of Planning & Transportation Commission meeting minutes from April 29, 2026
City Council administrative matters: schedule and assignments
